title: Biosafety Directive for New and Emerging Influenza A Viruses (Alphainfluenzavirus influenzae) - Canada.ca
content:
## Biosafety Directive for New and Emerging Influenza A Viruses ( Alphainfluenzavirus influenzae )
- Overview of the directive
- Who this directive is for
- Access the directive

## Overview of the directive
The Biosafety Directive for New and Emerging Influenza A Viruses (Alphainfluenzavirus influenzae) provides a comprehensive overview of the appropriate containment levels and additional operational practices for the handling of samples that may, are suspected to or are confirmed to contain a new or emerging strain of influenza A virus. This Directive has been revised to align its requirements and recommendations with updated content from the Canadian Biosafety Standard, Third Edition (CBS). This Directive is to be used in conjunction with the CBS.

## Who the directive is for
- Pathogen and Toxin Licence holders
- Terrestrial Animal Pathogen Import/Transfer Permit holders
- Biological Safety Officers
- Personnel in regulated facilities
- Personnel in facilities where diagnostic or surveillance activities are performed

## Access the directive
The directive is available on the Public Health Agency of Canada Training Portal. Access the directive
For questions, contact us by:
- phone at 1-613-957-1779
- email at pathogens.pathogenes@phac-aspc.gc.ca
